note: The single declared 3-process supervision / crash-recovery contract (tempdoc 627). For each supervised process and fault mode: the declared detection -> recovery -> terminal-state, plus guard(s) — a test that exercises the row, or an explicit sentinel where a unit test is infeasible (real OOM, Windows-only exit codes, process-suspend hangs). This is the 'single authority rather than scattered monitor/spawner logic' the tempdoc's Goal 2 asks for, covering BOTH the Worker (Body) and llama-server (Brain), including external-adoption. Asserted by SupervisionContractTest: (1) the per-process `policy` block must equal the live policy record defaults (SupervisionPolicy / BrainSupervisionPolicy) — drift fails the build; (2) every fault mode is present per process; (3) every guard resolves to a real test file or an allowed sentinel.

faultModes.recovery: respawn (no graceful stop — process already gone), shared restart budget

faultModes.detection: gRPC health-failure streak (KnowledgeServerBootstrap.checkHealth -> WorkerSpawner.recordHealthResult, 10s poll, threshold 3) while the process is alive
faultModes.recovery: graceful signal-bus stop then respawn, shared restart budget

faultModes.detection: 1s death monitor (OOM exits the JVM non-zero -> seen as process death; -XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError dumps)
faultModes.recovery: respawn (OOM is not distinguished from clean death by the decision)
faultModes.terminalState: capability DEGRADED + worker.restart_exhausted after cap (recurring OOM trips the cap)
faultModes.note: Decision treats OOM as clean death (exit code not inspected); SupervisionDecisionTest pins that mapping. A real OOM-loop -> give-up is a dev-stack smoke.

faultModes.detection: worker-side heartbeat suicide-pact (MmfWorkerSignalBus.shouldDie, 5s stale, 15s grace) corroborated by Head-PID liveness (WorkerLivenessDecision, tempdoc 630) + Windows Job Object KILL_ON_JOB_CLOSE
faultModes.recovery: worker self-exits when the heartbeat is stale AND Head's PID is dead (or no PID signal — standalone/legacy); OS force-kills on Head crash (Windows). Heartbeat-only on Linux/macOS where the Job Object is absent.
faultModes.terminalState: worker process exits (no orphan outliving a dead Head)
faultModes.livenessContinuity: tempdoc 630: a stale heartbeat is only actionable if Head was continuously live during the measurement window. A stale beat ALONE no longer kills — it must be corroborated by Head-PID death (WorkerLivenessDecision.HeadLiveness != ALIVE), because an OS suspend/resume produces a benign stale beat while Head (its PID persisting across suspend) is alive. UNKNOWN (no PID forwarded — standalone/tests) preserves the pre-630 heartbeat-only behavior. Guard: WorkerLivenessDecisionTest.
faultModes.note: Real-process coverage is opt-in systemTest (ChaosSuiteTest.workerDiesWhenHeartbeatStale) + WindowsJobObjectTest.assign_killsChildOnClose. The suspend/resume false-suicide is guarded fast in-process by WorkerLivenessDecisionTest (stale beat + Head ALIVE => no suicide).

faultModes.detection: awaitPort timeout (spawned but never published gRPC port)
faultModes.recovery: initial boot: start() throws -> capability DEGRADED + close(). Restart path: doRestart reaps the port-less orphan (stopProcess graceful); next supervision tick restarts or gives up from a clean slate.
faultModes.terminalState: no port-less zombie holding the data-dir lock; eventual give-up if it recurs
faultModes.note: The orphan reap lives in WorkerSpawner.doRestart's failure path; exercising it needs a real spawn that never publishes a port — a dev-stack smoke, not a unit test.

faultModes.recovery: restart with backoff (0ms first crash, crashRecoveryDelayMs after), reset on healthy restart
faultModes.terminalState: forceOffline -> Mode.OFFLINE + bounded failure-history after maxCrashes; capability OFFLINE via InferenceCapabilityWiring

faultModes.detection: periodic /health poll (LlamaServerOps.schedulePeriodicHealthCheck, periodicHealthIntervalMs, consecutiveFailuresBeforeRestart) while the process is alive
faultModes.recovery: managed: handleServerCrash (restart). external-adopted: goOfflineFromExternalFailure (no restart — no handle).

faultModes.detection: Process.waitFor crash monitor (OOM exits the server process -> same path as clean-exit crash)
faultModes.note: OOM is a process crash; LlamaServerOps.handleServerCrash handles it identically to clean-exit (exit code not distinguished). Covered by the clean-exit guard; a real GPU-OOM loop is a dev-stack smoke.

faultModes.detection: LlamaServerOps.waitForServerHealth: process exits during model load, or /health never ready before healthCheckTimeoutMs deadline (diagnosed via log-tail: arch mismatch / CUDA OOM / missing DLL / port-in-use)
faultModes.recovery: throw ModeTransitionException -> revert to prior mode (OFFLINE), failure diagnosed and recorded
faultModes.terminalState: OFFLINE with a typed startup-failure reason
faultModes.note: waitForServerHealth diagnosis path; a real partial boot (bad model / missing DLL / 120s timeout) is a dev-stack smoke — too slow/platform-specific for a unit test.

faultModes.detection: adoptExistingServerIfPresent on every start (health + /props probe, policy gates)
faultModes.recovery: adopt an externally-started server instead of spawning (no restart loop); health-only adoption behind a dev sysprop; admin policy can forbid external; an adopted server that fails health -> forced OFFLINE (cannot restart — no process handle)
faultModes.terminalState: OFFLINE on adopted-server failure (recovery deliberately declined — by design)
